acs.note: core safety invariant violated on the expired-reclaim path; TEST-003 only stresses a free scope
single-threaded compliant; its self-heal reclaim is what breaks AC-03 under concurrency

findings.title: Expired-reclaim TOCTOU: two acquirers can both win one scope
findings.detail: The self-heal branch does an unconditional rmSync(p,{force:true}) by path. A slow acquirer that observed the OLD expired lock can delete a NEW fresh lock a faster acquirer just created, then create its own -> two exit-0 winners. Violates Spec-AC-03 / contradicts D4. Demonstrated empirically via an injected legal-preemption delay (2 winners, owner-A clobbered).
findings.recommendation: Replace blind rmSync+retry with an atomic rename-steal: each acquirer renameSync(p -> unique aside name); only one succeeds per inode (others get ENOENT and re-loop and fail closed), the winner unlinks the aside file then retries openSync(p,'wx'). Keeps O_EXCL as the create-arbiter and makes reclaim single-winner.

findings.title: TEST-003 never exercises the expired-reclaim concurrency path
findings.detail: TEST-003 starts from an empty lock dir, so the rmSync self-heal branch is never entered; the validator stress likewise used free scopes. That is why E1 passed green.
findings.recommendation: Add a concurrency test that pre-seeds an expired lock, launches N acquires, asserts exactly one exit 0 and one lock file. Should be RED under the current code (with injected preemption) and GREEN after the E1 fix.

findings.title: safeScope is many-to-one; distinct scopes can collide to one lock file
findings.detail: a/b, a:b, a_b all -> a_b.lock, causing false contention or cross-scope wrong-owner. Low risk for disciplined ref-id scopes, unbounded for free-form scope strings. Path traversal itself is SAFE (separators sanitized; . / .. cannot escape LOCKS_DIR).
findings.recommendation: Append a short hash of the original scope to the filename, or detect collision (stored scope != requested) and fail 2.

findings.title: list output order is readdirSync (non-deterministic); sort by scope
release existsSync->readLock TOCTOU can turn an idempotent no-op into a spurious exit 4
reap(opts) called but function reap() ignores it; tidy for consistency
missing/non-numeric --ttl silently falls back to DEFAULT_TTL; consider exit 2
unchecked single writeSync; writeFileSync(fd,...) or return-value check is more defensive

expired_reclaim_toctou.reason: openSync('wx') arbitrates only the create; the rmSync self-heal reclaim is an unconditional unlink-by-path that is not serialized, allowing a fresh lock to be clobbered and a second acquirer to win. Demonstrated reachable via an injected legal scheduler preemption (two exit-0 winners).
